title: Critic-Generator Loop
constraints: The generator and critic must be separate agents
The loop must terminate when quality criteria are met or a max iteration count is reached
The critic must provide actionable feedback, not just a pass/fail
The generator must incorporate critic feedback in subsequent attempts
hints: Add a feedback loop where the critic's output is fed back to the generator as revision instructions
Include a quality check that decides whether to loop again or stop
In LangGraph, model this as a cyclic graph with a conditional edge based on critic approval

starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

generator = Agent(
    role="Content Generator",
    goal="Write a compelling product description",
    backstory="You are a creative copywriter.",
    llm=llm,
)

critic = Agent(
    role="Quality Critic",
    goal="Review content and provide improvement suggestions",
    backstory="You are a senior editor with high standards.",
    llm=llm,
)

# BUG: One-shot generation — the critic never reviews the output
# TODO: Add a review loop so the critic checks and the generator revises
generate_task = Task(
    description="Write a product description for {product}",
    expected_output="A compelling 100-word product description",
    agent=generator,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[generator],
    tasks=[generate_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"product": "wireless noise-cancelling headphones"})
print(result)
starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    product: str
    draft: str
    feedback: str
    approved: bool

def generate(state: State) -> dict:
    prompt = f"Write a compelling 100-word product description for: {state['product']}"
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    return {"draft": response.content}

# BUG: No critic node and no review loop exist
# TODO: Add a critic node that reviews the draft and a conditional loop

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("generate", generate)
graph.add_edge(START, "generate")
graph.add_edge("generate", END)

app = graph.compile()
result = app.invoke({"product": "wireless noise-cancelling headphones", "draft": "", "feedback": "", "approved": False})
print(result["draft"])
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

generator = Agent(
    name="Content Generator",
    instructions="You are a creative copywriter. Write compelling product descriptions.",
)

critic = Agent(
    name="Quality Critic",
    instructions="You are a senior editor with high standards. Review content and provide improvement suggestions.",
)

# BUG: One-shot generation — the critic never reviews the output
# TODO: Add a review loop so the critic checks and the generator revises
result = Runner.run_sync(
    generator,
    "Write a compelling 100-word product description for wireless noise-cancelling headphones",
)
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Review loop implemented
evaluationCriteria.description: The generator and critic run in a loop where feedback leads to revision

evaluationCriteria.label: Critic provides actionable feedback
evaluationCriteria.description: The critic agent reviews the draft and returns specific improvement suggestions

evaluationCriteria.label: Generator incorporates feedback
evaluationCriteria.description: The generator uses the critic's feedback to produce an improved draft

evaluationCriteria.label: Loop terminates correctly
evaluationCriteria.description: The loop stops when quality is met or a maximum iteration count is reached
